---
title: "Residual Value Risk — R Replication"
author: "Nisrina Walyadin"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

## Overview

This notebook replicates the core depreciation model and key visualisations
from the Python analysis in R, demonstrating cross-tool flexibility.

```{r install, eval=FALSE}
# Run once
#install.packages(c("tidyverse", "broom", "scales", "ggridges", "viridis"))
```

```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(scales)
library(viridis)

theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 13))
```

## 1. Load Data

```{r load}
# Load the cleaned dataset produced by Python notebook 01
df <- read_csv("data/vehicles_clean.csv")

cat("Rows:", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Columns:", ncol(df), "\n")
glimpse(df)
```

## 2. Depreciation Model (OLS)

```{r model}
# Log-linear OLS — mirrors the Python statsmodels specification
model_formula <- log_price ~ vehicle_age + log_odometer + is_ev

# Add brand if available
if ("brand" %in% colnames(df)) {
  # Keep top 15 brands
  top_brands <- df %>%
    count(brand, sort = TRUE) %>%
    slice_head(n = 15) %>%
    pull(brand)

  df_model <- df %>%
    filter(brand %in% top_brands) %>%
    drop_na(log_price, vehicle_age, log_odometer, is_ev, brand)

  model_formula <- log_price ~ vehicle_age + log_odometer + is_ev + brand
} else {
  df_model <- df %>%
    drop_na(log_price, vehicle_age, log_odometer, is_ev)
}

ols_model <- lm(model_formula, data = df_model)

# Clean coefficient table
tidy(ols_model) %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.05) %>%
  mutate(
    estimate   = round(estimate, 4),
    std.error  = round(std.error, 4),
    p.value    = scales::pvalue(p.value)
  ) %>%
  print()

cat("\nR-squared:", round(summary(ols_model)$r.squared, 3))
cat("\nAdj R-squared:", round(summary(ols_model)$adj.r.squared, 3), "\n")
```

```{r interpret}
coefs <- coef(ols_model)

age_effect <- (exp(coefs["vehicle_age"]) - 1) * 100
ev_effect  <- (exp(coefs["is_ev"]) - 1) * 100

cat(sprintf("Age effect: each additional year → %+.1f%% change in price\n", age_effect))
cat(sprintf("EV premium/discount vs ICE: %+.1f%%\n", ev_effect))
```

## 3. Visualisations

### 3a. Depreciation Curve by Fuel Type

```{r plot_depreciation, fig.width=11, fig.height=6}
df_model <- df_model %>%
  mutate(
    price_actual = exp(log_price),
    fuel_label   = if_else(is_ev == 1, "EV", "ICE")
  )

# Median price by age and fuel type
age_summary <- df_model %>%
  group_by(vehicle_age, fuel_label) %>%
  summarise(median_price = median(price_actual), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(age_summary, aes(x = vehicle_age, y = median_price, color = fuel_label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_dollar(prefix = "€", suffix = "k",
                                            scale = 1/1000, accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("EV" = "#2E86AB", "ICE" = "#E84855")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Residual Value by Vehicle Age & Fuel Type",
    subtitle = "Median market price — EV vs ICE depreciation curves",
    x        = "Vehicle Age (years)",
    y        = "Median Market Price",
    color    = "Fuel Type"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey50"),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

ggsave("outputs/R_01_depreciation_curve.png", width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 150)
cat("💾 Saved: outputs/R_01_depreciation_curve.png\n")
```

### 3b. Predicted vs Actual

```{r plot_pred_actual, fig.width=8, fig.height=8}
df_model$price_pred <- exp(predict(ols_model, df_model))

# Sample for readability
sample_df <- df_model %>% sample_n(min(4000, nrow(df_model)))

ggplot(sample_df, aes(x = price_actual, y = price_pred)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15, size = 1, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red",
              linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_dollar(prefix = "€", suffix = "k",
                                            scale = 1/1000, accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_dollar(prefix = "€", suffix = "k",
                                            scale = 1/1000, accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Predicted vs Actual Market Value",
    subtitle = "OLS log-linear model — red dashed line = perfect prediction",
    x        = "Actual Price",
    y        = "Predicted Price"
  ) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))

ggsave("outputs/R_02_predicted_vs_actual.png", width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 150)
cat("💾 Saved: outputs/R_02_predicted_vs_actual.png\n")
```

### 3c. Risk Exposure by Age Band

```{r plot_risk, fig.width=11, fig.height=6}
df_risk <- df_model %>%
  mutate(
    guaranteed_rv  = exp(log_price) / 0.85 / (0.90 ^ pmax(vehicle_age - 1, 0)) * 0.50,
    at_risk        = price_actual < guaranteed_rv,
    risk_exposure  = pmax(guaranteed_rv - price_actual, 0),
    age_band       = cut(vehicle_age,
                         breaks = c(0, 2, 4, 6, 10, Inf),
                         labels = c("0–2 yrs", "3–4 yrs", "5–6 yrs",
                                    "7–10 yrs", "10+ yrs"),
                         right  = TRUE)
  )

risk_summary <- df_risk %>%
  group_by(age_band, fuel_label) %>%
  summarise(
    pct_at_risk      = mean(at_risk, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    avg_exposure     = mean(risk_exposure, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups          = "drop"
  )

ggplot(risk_summary, aes(x = age_band, y = pct_at_risk, fill = fuel_label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pct_at_risk)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("EV" = "#2E86AB", "ICE" = "#E84855")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  labs(
    title    = "% Contracts at Residual Value Risk by Age Band & Fuel Type",
    subtitle = "At risk = predicted market value < guaranteed buyback price (50% of new)",
    x        = "Vehicle Age Band",
    y        = "% Contracts at Risk",
    fill     = "Fuel Type"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(color = "grey50"),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

ggsave("outputs/R_03_risk_by_age_band.png", width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 150)
cat("💾 Saved: outputs/R_03_risk_by_age_band.png\n")
cat("\n✅ R replication complete — all outputs saved to outputs/\n")
```
